title: centaurea mosana
intro: Centaurea mosana, also known as wild artichoke or star thistle, is a perennial herb native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its small yellow flowers and edible leaves. The leaves and flowers of Centaurea mosana have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to treat digestive issues, wounds, and skin conditions. The plant is rich in vitamins A and C, potassium, and fiber, providing numerous health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. Centaurea mosana can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used as a food ingredient in salads and soups.
